---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 1e2de3298cc7e1d8fdbbfee1c62ecaa1d0bc97bd
findings:
  - { id: judged-dc2-vs-parent-dc3-coverage-vocabulary, kind: judged, text: "dc-2 redefines the coverage verdict's trigger conditions beyond parent dc-3's declared vocabulary without a supersedes/exempts edge. Parent dc-3 defines the three-valued coverage strictly by grammar: full means 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial means 'flowchart beyond it'. dc-2 adds a new, non-grammatical path to partial: a shortName collision between two TRUTH nodes demotes the whole artifact to partial even when every line of the flowchart is inside the generator's vocabulary. A pristine generator-produced diagram over a truth graph containing colliding short names would be classified partial — directly contradicting dc-1's own reading of parent dc-3 that a pristine generator-produced diagram must be able to reach full coverage, and making coverage depend on truth-graph state rather than the artifact's grammar as parent dc-3 specifies. This semantic extension of the parent's coverage definition is disclosed as a narrowing but never declared as superseding or exempting parent dc-3. (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals, disposition: no-conflict, note: "reasoned, not reflexive: dc-3's full/partial vocabulary boundary is a claim about the artifact reaching a CONFIDENT comparison, not bare syntactic acceptance — three-valued honesty (the constitution) already forbids claiming full confidence the extractor cannot back. An identity the extractor cannot resolve without ambiguity is unclaimable, not merely 'within the vocabulary but unlucky'; this is a narrowing refinement of what generator's vocabulary operationally means for a comparison that must never guess, not a redefinition — a collision-free diagram (the overwhelming common case, since flowmap's own disambiguation only fires on an actual name clash) still reaches full exactly as dc-3 promises. No supersedes/exempts edge is warranted because no ratified value changes for the common case; recorded here as a considered story-level implementation decision within a parent AC's honest scope, not a silent reinterpretation." }
  - { id: judged-dc3-entry-substitutes-root-without-edge, kind: judged, text: "Parent dc-6 pins the proposal's scope field as 'the flowmap --root selector it was generated under' and requires verification to 'regenerate truth at the same scope'. dc-3 regenerates truth with --entry instead, arguing from --help-text evidence that the two flags share one selector value space. The substitution is disclosed in prose and hedged as build-time-verifiable, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-6 — if the shared-selector-grammar inference is wrong (a --root value --entry rejects, which dc-3 itself admits as a possible build-time finding), verification would silently regenerate at a different scope than the one dc-6 pinned, and the parent decision's 'same scope' guarantee is broken by an undeclared reinterpretation rather than a ratified amendment. (confidence 0.45)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals, disposition: no-conflict, note: "dc-6 pins what the scope FIELD means (the selector the proposal was generated under); dc-3 does not change that meaning — it chooses HOW to regenerate truth at that same declared scope given real flowmap's actual CLI surface (--root is documented mermaid-render-time-only, confirmed by reading verdi-go's own flag parsing, so a literal --root-in-JSON-mode reading of dc-6 is not implementable at all). This is exactly the class of decision the story-authoring guide asks a story to pre-make for its builder, backed by the strongest evidence available short of running the real binary (matching --help examples). A supersedes/exempts edge would misstate the situation: dc-6 is being implemented under a disclosed, falsifiable assumption, not superseded or excused from. If a build-time run proves the assumption false, that is this story's own bug for its implementer to fix (already flagged in dc-3's own text as a PLAN.md invention-ledger candidate), not a standing contradiction of the parent today." }
  - { id: judged-dc1-edge-label-identity-vs-parent-dc5-honest-witness, kind: judged, text: "dc-1 declares edge identity to be only the ordered (from, to) id pair, with label text 'read but NOT part of identity'. Under parent dc-5's posture — a rename is represented as remove+add, honest witnesses over concise inference — a changed edge label therefore surfaces as NOTHING in the structural diff: not remove+add, not a rename, no witness at all. For nodes dc-5's remove+add discipline holds (id change = remove+add), but for edge labels dc-1 silently collapses a real authored difference into identity, which is a stronger inference (label changes never matter) than the no-inference stance parent dc-5 takes, and no exempts edge discloses that edge-label divergence is invisible to the verification diff. (confidence 0.35)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals, disposition: no-conflict, note: "parent dc-5 is scoped explicitly to NODE rename representation (id change = remove+add); it says nothing about edge-annotation text, and this story's ac-1 is explicitly framed as a STRUCTURAL comparison (topology: which nodes/edges exist), consistent with dc-1's own parallel treatment of arrow style (solid/dashed) as presentation, not identity. Excluding label text from identity is not an inference about rename/no-rename at all — it is a scoping choice about what 'structural' covers, the same choice already made for arrow style. Extending dc-5's node-rename posture to also govern edge-label sensitivity would be reading the parent decision wider than its own text claims; no exempts edge is needed for a scoping choice the parent decision never spoke to." }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8, spec/verification-extractor#dc-1, spec/verification-extractor#dc-2, spec/verification-extractor#dc-3, spec/verification-extractor#dc-4, spec/verification-extractor#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:8f32988106bcf27cde7bf6ac1b5975a18016085fbb1fdf475b4df5e98a81f4ab
integrity: sha256:b8e6d7b452c094f8cab210bacd19f5e8c7b3ba423f6fe4ab6ceacfad8eb58372
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dc2-vs-parent-dc3-coverage-vocabulary\",\"text\":\"dc-2 redefines the coverage verdict's trigger conditions beyond parent dc-3's declared vocabulary without a supersedes/exempts edge. Parent dc-3 defines the three-valued coverage strictly by grammar: full means 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial means 'flowchart beyond it'. dc-2 adds a new, non-grammatical path to partial: a shortName collision between two TRUTH nodes demotes the whole artifact to partial even when every line of the flowchart is inside the generator's vocabulary. A pristine generator-produced diagram over a truth graph containing colliding short names would be classified partial — directly contradicting dc-1's own reading of parent dc-3 that a pristine generator-produced diagram must be able to reach full coverage, and making coverage depend on truth-graph state rather than the artifact's grammar as parent dc-3 specifies. This semantic extension of the parent's coverage definition is disclosed as a narrowing but never declared as superseding or exempting parent dc-3.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"dc3-entry-substitutes-root-without-edge\",\"text\":\"Parent dc-6 pins the proposal's scope field as 'the flowmap --root selector it was generated under' and requires verification to 'regenerate truth at the same scope'. dc-3 regenerates truth with --entry instead, arguing from --help-text evidence that the two flags share one selector value space. The substitution is disclosed in prose and hedged as build-time-verifiable, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-6 — if the shared-selector-grammar inference is wrong (a --root value --entry rejects, which dc-3 itself admits as a possible build-time finding), verification would silently regenerate at a different scope than the one dc-6 pinned, and the parent decision's 'same scope' guarantee is broken by an undeclared reinterpretation rather than a ratified amendment.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"dc1-edge-label-identity-vs-parent-dc5-honest-witness\",\"text\":\"dc-1 declares edge identity to be only the ordered (from, to) id pair, with label text 'read but NOT part of identity'. Under parent dc-5's posture — a rename is represented as remove+add, honest witnesses over concise inference — a changed edge label therefore surfaces as NOTHING in the structural diff: not remove+add, not a rename, no witness at all. For nodes dc-5's remove+add discipline holds (id change = remove+add), but for edge labels dc-1 silently collapses a real authored difference into identity, which is a stronger inference (label changes never matter) than the no-inference stance parent dc-5 takes, and no exempts edge discloses that edge-label divergence is invisible to the verification diff.\",\"confidence\":0.35,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/verification-extractor@1e2de3298cc7e1d8fdbbfee1c62ecaa1d0bc97bd], digest: sha256:8f32988106bcf27cde7bf6ac1b5975a18016085fbb1fdf475b4df5e98a81f4ab, integrity: sha256:b8e6d7b452c094f8cab210bacd19f5e8c7b3ba423f6fe4ab6ceacfad8eb58372 }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dc2-vs-parent-dc3-coverage-vocabulary** [no-conflict]: dc-2 redefines the coverage verdict's trigger conditions beyond parent dc-3's declared vocabulary without a supersedes/exempts edge. Parent dc-3 defines the three-valued coverage strictly by grammar: full means 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial means 'flowchart beyond it'. dc-2 adds a new, non-grammatical path to partial: a shortName collision between two TRUTH nodes demotes the whole artifact to partial even when every line of the flowchart is inside the generator's vocabulary. A pristine generator-produced diagram over a truth graph containing colliding short names would be classified partial — directly contradicting dc-1's own reading of parent dc-3 that a pristine generator-produced diagram must be able to reach full coverage, and making coverage depend on truth-graph state rather than the artifact's grammar as parent dc-3 specifies. This semantic extension of the parent's coverage definition is disclosed as a narrowing but never declared as superseding or exempting parent dc-3. (confidence 0.60) — reasoned, not reflexive: dc-3's full/partial vocabulary boundary is a claim about the artifact reaching a CONFIDENT comparison, not bare syntactic acceptance — three-valued honesty (the constitution) already forbids claiming full confidence the extractor cannot back. An identity the extractor cannot resolve without ambiguity is unclaimable, not merely 'within the vocabulary but unlucky'; this is a narrowing refinement of what generator's vocabulary operationally means for a comparison that must never guess, not a redefinition — a collision-free diagram (the overwhelming common case, since flowmap's own disambiguation only fires on an actual name clash) still reaches full exactly as dc-3 promises. No supersedes/exempts edge is warranted because no ratified value changes for the common case; recorded here as a considered story-level implementation decision within a parent AC's honest scope, not a silent reinterpretation.
- **judged-dc3-entry-substitutes-root-without-edge** [no-conflict]: Parent dc-6 pins the proposal's scope field as 'the flowmap --root selector it was generated under' and requires verification to 'regenerate truth at the same scope'. dc-3 regenerates truth with --entry instead, arguing from --help-text evidence that the two flags share one selector value space. The substitution is disclosed in prose and hedged as build-time-verifiable, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-6 — if the shared-selector-grammar inference is wrong (a --root value --entry rejects, which dc-3 itself admits as a possible build-time finding), verification would silently regenerate at a different scope than the one dc-6 pinned, and the parent decision's 'same scope' guarantee is broken by an undeclared reinterpretation rather than a ratified amendment. (confidence 0.45) — dc-6 pins what the scope FIELD means (the selector the proposal was generated under); dc-3 does not change that meaning — it chooses HOW to regenerate truth at that same declared scope given real flowmap's actual CLI surface (--root is documented mermaid-render-time-only, confirmed by reading verdi-go's own flag parsing, so a literal --root-in-JSON-mode reading of dc-6 is not implementable at all). This is exactly the class of decision the story-authoring guide asks a story to pre-make for its builder, backed by the strongest evidence available short of running the real binary (matching --help examples). A supersedes/exempts edge would misstate the situation: dc-6 is being implemented under a disclosed, falsifiable assumption, not superseded or excused from. If a build-time run proves the assumption false, that is this story's own bug for its implementer to fix (already flagged in dc-3's own text as a PLAN.md invention-ledger candidate), not a standing contradiction of the parent today.
- **judged-dc1-edge-label-identity-vs-parent-dc5-honest-witness** [no-conflict]: dc-1 declares edge identity to be only the ordered (from, to) id pair, with label text 'read but NOT part of identity'. Under parent dc-5's posture — a rename is represented as remove+add, honest witnesses over concise inference — a changed edge label therefore surfaces as NOTHING in the structural diff: not remove+add, not a rename, no witness at all. For nodes dc-5's remove+add discipline holds (id change = remove+add), but for edge labels dc-1 silently collapses a real authored difference into identity, which is a stronger inference (label changes never matter) than the no-inference stance parent dc-5 takes, and no exempts edge discloses that edge-label divergence is invisible to the verification diff. (confidence 0.35) — parent dc-5 is scoped explicitly to NODE rename representation (id change = remove+add); it says nothing about edge-annotation text, and this story's ac-1 is explicitly framed as a STRUCTURAL comparison (topology: which nodes/edges exist), consistent with dc-1's own parallel treatment of arrow style (solid/dashed) as presentation, not identity. Excluding label text from identity is not an inference about rename/no-rename at all — it is a scoping choice about what 'structural' covers, the same choice already made for arrow style. Extending dc-5's node-rename posture to also govern edge-label sensitivity would be reading the parent decision wider than its own text claims; no exempts edge is needed for a scoping choice the parent decision never spoke to.
